reason: Select the backend checklists needed to review runtime, persistence, lifecycle, security, logging, and quality compliance.
Select the frontend checklists needed to review platform abstraction, repository-state isolation, event typing, accessibility, and quality compliance.
Review delivered behavior against confirmed requirements, stable acceptance IDs, compatibility surfaces, and explicit non-goals.
Review implementation against the authoritative architecture, active/candidate transaction model, generation rules, OperationCoordinator, and feasibility-gate decisions.

Audit the approved 14-child DAG, dependency/start/rollback gates, and exact AC-to-DES-to-child-to-validation/evidence trace matrix; this curated context identifies required evidence but is not itself a test-evidence ledger.
Confirm implementation remains within the accepted Wails 3 production desktop decision.
Confirm the hybrid transport, independent feasibility gate, continuous ACK, out-of-order handling, cancellation, and bounded backpressure contracts are satisfied.
Confirm launcher handoff, transaction-scoped rollback, anti-downgrade behavior, and data-format fence are satisfied.
Compare observable Web, PWA, Docker, API, database, scheduler, and streaming behavior with the recorded compatibility baseline.
Check pinned dependency tuple and framework assumptions against the recorded primary-source assessment; prototype outputs and executed tests must be supplied separately as evidence.
